# Abstract
We design a high efficiency hybrid converter for electric drives. The topology reduces switching losses at medium loads.

# Introduction
Power conversion efficiency is critical for industrial drives. Conventional converters trade efficiency for cost.

# Methods
A hybrid full bridge with resonant switching was developed. Control uses a dual loop to regulate current and voltage.

# Results
Measured efficiency peaks at 97.2 percent and exceeds 95 percent from 20 to 80 percent load. Thermal rise is reduced by 8 degrees C.

Figure 1. Hybrid converter topology and control loops.

# Discussion
The design simplifies magnetics while maintaining low ripple. Component tolerance impacts switching transitions.

Table 1. Efficiency comparison with baseline converters.

# Conclusion
The hybrid design achieves high efficiency across operating ranges. Future work will target higher power density.
